preamble: Labial palatalization in Bantu languages can result in the nasal [m] shifting to an affricate [dʒ], often through intermediate palatalized stages.

examples.note: Common shift in some Bantu languages where labials become palatal affricates before high-front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
